sections.paragraph: (“strong,” “fortified,” as if Valentia) pr.n. [Gaza, Azzah] (LXX. Γάζα), one of the five cities of the Philistines, Jos 11:22 Jud. 16:1 , 21 Jdg 16:21 1Sa 6:17 Jer 25:20 Amo 1:6, Zep 2:4 a royal city ( Zec 9:5), situated on the southern borders of Palestine ( Gen 10:19 1Ki 5:4), taken by the Jews in the time of the Judges ( Jud 1:18), but soon after recovered again by the Philistines. It is frequently mentioned by the Greek writers, of whom Plutarch calls it the greatest city of Syria; Arrian calls it a great city, situated on a lofty place, and well fortified. It even now retains its ancient name (غزّة). Its history is given at considerable length by Reland, in Palæstina, p. 788-800. Gent. n. עֲזָּתִי Jud. 16:2 .
